body:
🔴 **Design Issue** — Redundant `OperationGroup` in client property name

`readonly updateSummariesOperationGroup: UpdateSummariesOperationGroupOperations;`

The client property `updateSummariesOperationGroup` contains the redundant suffix `OperationGroup` — all properties on a service client are already understood to be operation groups, so the suffix adds noise and is inconsistent with the rest of the client (e.g., `updateSummaries`, `updates`, `clusters`).

This likely stems from a naming conflict in the TypeSpec definition where two operation groups share the `UpdateSummaries` prefix. The second group (`checkHealth`, `checkUpdates`) should be renamed to a more meaningful concept (e.g., `updateSummariesActions` or merged into the existing `UpdateSummaries` group if the service model permits).

**Fix:** Rename the TypeSpec operation group `UpdateSummariesOperationGroup` to a name without the `OperationGroup` suffix, then regenerate. File an issue in [autorest.typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues) if the emitter causes the redundant suffix.

1 design issue found in this PR for `@azure/arm-azurestackhci@4.1.0-beta.1`. All tooling checks (CHANGELOG comparison base, API version progression, userAgent alignment, KnownVersions) are clean. The single finding is a redundant `OperationGroup` suffix in the new `updateSummariesOperationGroup` client property name, which needs to be addressed in the TypeSpec source before release.
